key_departures.impact: Released by the Commanders before free agency and promptly signed a 3-year, $30M deal with the Chargers. Biadasz started all 16 games in 2025 and was the anchor of a top-tier offensive line. His release creates a direct need at center — Washington has no proven starter at the position. Nick Allegretti is the internal candidate to compete for the role.

Released by Washington, saving $18.5M in cap space. Lattimore never delivered on his promise after being acquired via trade in 2024 — he played just 2 games in 2024 (hamstring) and 9 in 2025 (torn ACL in November). His exit escalates an already critical CB need. Washington is left with only Trey Amos (broken leg, 2025 rookie) and Mike Sainristil as guaranteed roster CBs heading into free agency.

Samuel's contract voided at the start of the new league year, triggering a $12.4M dead cap hit. He led the team with 727+ receiving yards in 2025 despite the team's 5-12 collapse. His departure creates a significant void at the WR2/slot role opposite Terry McLaurin. Washington has not yet replaced him with a comparable signing, leaving WR a critical unaddressed need.

Ertz is a UFA after posting 504 receiving yards (third on team) in 2025 before suffering a late-season knee injury. He is not expected to return at 36, and his exit leaves Washington with Ben Sinnott as the lone returning TE option of note. Creates or escalates a TE need.

Wagner was not re-signed after two productive seasons in Washington. His departure opens a massive hole at LB2 alongside Frankie Luvu (also in a contract year). While the Commanders moved quickly to sign Leo Chenal to fill this void, Wagner's exit was the catalyst for that action.

Miller led the Commanders with 9 sacks in 2025 on a one-year deal at age 36 — a remarkable performance, but clearly a non-recurring situation. His departure leaves a major gap at EDGE that the Oweh signing directly addresses. Washington also has Dorance Armstrong recovering from a torn ACL.

Starting left guard Paul departed as a UFA. He was a quality starter on Washington's offensive line and his exit compounds the IOL need created by Biadasz's release — now Washington potentially needs both a center and a left guard.

signings.analysis: Historic extension — Tunsil becomes the first offensive lineman at $30M/year, with $61.5M in guarantees including a record $32.5M signing bonus for an OT. This is the most fully guaranteed deal Washington could have given. Tunsil is a 5x Pro Bowler protecting Jayden Daniels through 2028. LT is off the board entirely. No draft capital needed here.

Washington's marquee splash of the offseason. Oweh (27) is a 2021 first-round pick who tallied 17.5 sacks over the past two combined seasons and had a monster playoff game (3 sacks, 2 forced fumbles) with the Chargers. The 4-year/$100M deal with $68M guaranteed is a massive bet on an ascending player. With Dorance Armstrong returning from ACL (depth/rotational) and Deatrich Wise/Drake Jackson re-signed as depth, EDGE goes from a Tier 1 need to essentially addressed. Washington may still want a complementary rusher via the draft, but the starter need is done.

Robertson (2-year/$16M, $9.3M gtd) adds a versatile CB who can play slot or outside, giving new DC Daronte Jones a chess piece for his coverage disguises. He had 10 starts with Detroit in 2025. At $8M/year, this is below starter money on the positional market — a solid addition, not a needle-mover. Washington entered FA with only Trey Amos and Mike Sainristil as guaranteed CB roster players. Robertson is CB3/flex at best. CB remains a real need — the team explicitly noted they need to keep adding at this position.

A homecoming for the former Washington 5th-round pick (2018), Settle returns on a 3-year/$24M deal (up to $25.5M with incentives). He was a full-time starter for Houston in 2025 and graded as the 10th-best pass-rushing DT. He slots in alongside Daron Payne and Javon Kinlaw to give Washington a legitimate three-deep at IDL. However, Washington's interior still generated just 8 sacks and 20 QB hits from the DT group in 2025 — Settle's one sack/one QB hit in 2025 (foot injury) doesn't fully solve interior pass rush. IDL is partially addressed; a developmental piece in the draft could still help. Guarantees TBD per public reporting — estimated ~$10M based on AAV structure.

Washington went well above Chenal's projected market (~2yr/$11M) to land the 25-year-old 2x Super Bowl champion on a 3-year/$24.75M deal. Chenal started 44 games over four seasons with Kansas City, posted a 9.99 Relative Athletic Score, and recorded 193 tackles, 7 sacks and 28 pressures in his career. He gives new DC Daronte Jones a flexible piece who can play off-ball, rush the passer, or even line up as a stand-up DT. With Frankie Luvu also retained, LB goes from a critical need (post-Wagner) to a legitimate strength. Guarantees TBD — estimated ~$13M based on AAV and 3-year commitment.

Third consecutive one-year deal (1yr/$7M, up to $11M with incentives) for Mariota as Jayden Daniels' backup. Mariota started 8 of 11 games in 2025 due to Daniels' injuries, throwing for 1,695 yards, 10 TDs and 7 INTs. This is an important depth retention — given Daniels' injury history, this is far more than a routine backup signing. But it doesn't affect QB need tiers since Daniels is the unquestioned starter.

Wise re-signed on a 1-year deal (terms undisclosed — believed to be near veteran minimum) before the legal tampering window. He played only 2 games in 2025 due to injury but provides veteran leadership and rotational value behind Oweh and Armstrong. Low-impact retention; no change to need tier for EDGE.

Jackson (25) is a high-upside pass rusher who played only 3 games in 2025 in his return from a torn ACL. Re-signed on a 1-year deal (terms undisclosed) to provide developmental depth. He has starter upside long-term but this is purely a depth signing for 2026. Does not affect need tiers.

Tuttle re-signed on a 1-year deal (terms undisclosed). He is an 8-year veteran with 107 career games and 66 starts. Provides run-stopping depth alongside Payne, Kinlaw, and Settle. Low-impact depth retention.

Wylie re-signed on a 2-year/$7.5M deal as interior line depth and veteran insurance. He has played OT and G and has familiarity at C. At $3.75M/year, this is pure depth money. With Biadasz and Chris Paul gone, Wylie is an internal option at center in a pinch, but he is not a starter-caliber solution. IOL remains a critical need.

Allegretti's existing contract was restructured into a new 2-year extension (exact terms undisclosed; structured to lower his 2026 cap hit per reporting). He is the primary internal candidate to compete for the starting center job vacated by Biadasz. He has experience at center but is not a proven starter at the position. IOL remains a significant need regardless — Washington still needs to add a genuine starting center via FA or the draft.

Burks re-signed on a 1-year/$4M deal as WR depth. He caught 10 passes for 130 yards on 22 targets in 2025 — not enough to be relied upon as a meaningful contributor. This is a low-cost depth retention; WR remains a critical need opposite Terry McLaurin.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Oweh signing (4yr/$100M, $68M gtd) at age 27 is a franchise-caliber investment that fully addresses the starter need. Armstrong returns from ACL as a complementary piece; Wise and Jackson provide rotation depth. Washington may still draft a developmental EDGE on Day 2/3, but it is no longer a premium draft need. Drop from Tier 1 critical to Tier 3 luxury/depth.

Tunsil extension (2yr/$60.2M, $61.5M gtd, $30.1M AAV) locks up the best LT in the franchise's recent history through 2028. Josh Conerly Jr. holds the RT spot. OT is fully settled — no draft need at this position whatsoever.

Chenal signing (3yr/$24.75M) fills the Bobby Wagner void with a 25-year-old ascending starter who has Championship-game pedigree and elite athleticism. Frankie Luvu provides a solid complement. Washington went significantly over market to land Chenal. LB drops from a top need to a depth/developmental consideration — a Day 3 pick to groom behind Chenal and Luvu would be ideal but not urgent.

The combination of Biadasz's release (signed with Chargers 3yr/$30M) and Chris Paul's departure to free agency leaves Washington without a proven starter at center OR left guard. Allegretti and Wylie provide internal competition but neither is a reliable Tier 1 option at center. This is now the most critical unaddressed need on offense. Should be a top draft or mid-FA target (Tyler Linderbaum still being pursued). Escalate to Tier 1.

Deebo Samuel (led team with 727+ receiving yards) departed via contract void. Washington missed on Romeo Doubs (chose Patriots). Terry McLaurin remains but is in the final year of his deal and his future is uncertain beyond 2026. Burks re-signing is pure depth. Washington has no legitimate WR2 on the roster. This is now tied for the most urgent offensive need alongside IOL/C. Escalate to Tier 1.

Robertson signing (2yr/$16M, $9.3M gtd) adds a useful flex CB who can play inside or outside, reducing but not eliminating the CB need. Washington still has only Robertson, Sainristil, and Amos as genuine CBs. CB is explicitly still a need by team's own admission. Remains a high-priority draft or FA target — drop from Tier 1 to Tier 2.

Settle signing (3yr/$24M) alongside Payne, Kinlaw, and Tuttle gives Washington legitimate depth at IDL for the first time in years. However, interior pass rush was abysmal in 2025 (8 sacks, 20 QB hits from the DT group) and Settle himself had just 1 sack last season. This group is better but not fixed — drafting an IDL in Day 2/3 still makes sense. Drop from Tier 2 to Tier 3.

Zach Ertz (504 receiving yards in 2025, third on team) departed and is not expected to return at age 36 following a knee injury. Ben Sinnott is the only returning TE and has been 'largely invisible in the passing game' per team observers. Washington has not made any TE signing. This is an unaddressed gap that directly impacts Jayden Daniels' receiving options. Escalate to Tier 1.

Washington has only Jacory Croskey-Merritt under contract (with RFA Chris Rodriguez Jr. eligible for a tender). No RB signing has been made in free agency despite this being a stated team need. Kenneth Walker III and other RB targets remain on the market. RB stays at Tier 2 — the team still needs a true RB1.

No safety signings confirmed. Washington has Jeremy Reaves and Will Harris but lacks a coverage-capable starting safety for Daronte Jones's new scheme. Safety need is unchanged — still a Tier 2 priority.

key_additions.headline: 27-year-old ascending pass rusher (17.5 sacks last 2 seasons) becomes Washington's new defensive cornerstone and highest-paid player
Record-breaking OT extension ($30.1M AAV) locks up Jayden Daniels' blindside protector through 2028
25-year-old 2x Super Bowl champion fills Bobby Wagner's vacancy with elite athleticism (9.99 RAS) and 44 career starts
Former Washington 5th-round pick returns home to bolster interior D-line with pass-rush upside alongside Daron Payne
Versatile slot/outside CB adds a third rostered cornerback to a depleted secondary in need of bodies
Veteran backup returns for a third season as Jayden Daniels' insurance policy after starting 8 games in 2025

key_losses.headline: Released starting center departs for LA, leaving Washington without a proven starter at the position and creating a critical IOL need
Ill-fated trade acquisition (cost 3rd/4th/6th round picks) cut loose after ACL tear; departure escalates already thin CB room
Rental WR1 who led team in 2025 receiving yards departs via contract void, leaving Washington with no proven WR2 opposite McLaurin
Veteran TE (504 yards in 2025) unlikely to return at 36 after knee injury, leaving Ben Sinnott as lone TE option
Future Hall of Famer not retained after two productive seasons; exit triggered the Chenal signing to fill the void
37-year-old led team with 9 sacks in 2025 but was always a one-year stopgap; Oweh signing is his long-term replacement
Starting LG departed in free agency, compounding the Biadasz loss and leaving Washington short at multiple IOL spots
